description: All foundation, site, permit, material, and crew prerequisites verified on the ground before the first stick of lumber is stood — nothing starts until every item closes.

items.label: Baldwin County building permit posted on-site in weatherproof holder
Approved permit set (stamped drawings) physically on-site and accessible to inspectors
Septic permit approved and copy on file (or sewer tap fee paid and receipt on file)
Electrical and plumbing permits approved and posted or on file per Baldwin County requirement
Any HOA or coastal setback approval documents received and on file
Flood zone determination confirmed; if AE or VE zone, first-floor elevation verified against LOMA/BFE requirement and noted in file
Baldwin County foundation/footing inspection passed and inspection card signed off
Slab or stem-wall concrete cylinder break results on file, minimum PSI per structural engineer's spec achieved
Slab flatness tolerance verified — maximum 3/16" in 10 ft for living areas per spec
All anchor bolts (or strap embed locations) mapped against structural engineer's anchor schedule; any deviations documented and EOR-approved in writing
Foundation dimensions field-verified against architectural plans: overall footprint, bearing wall centerlines, garage slab drop, step-downs at covered porches
Slab curing complete — minimum 7 days (or engineer's specified cure time) before framing load applied
All slab penetrations (plumbing rough stubs, conduit sleeves) confirmed at correct location per MEP plans and protected/capped
Erosion controls (silt fence, inlet protection) inspected and intact before framing activity increases disturbed area
Temporary construction entrance (min. 6" compacted gravel) in place to prevent tracked mud on public road
Grade around foundation confirmed positive drainage away from slab on all sides (min. 6" drop in first 10 ft per IRC R401.3)
Dumpster and debris staging area established and not blocking material delivery or crane path
Temporary power (100A min. construction meter) energized and GFCI-protected outlets functional
Portable restroom on-site and positioned per OSHA 29 CFR 1926.51 proximity requirements
Lumber package delivered, quantity verified against framing takeoff, and inspected for species/grade matching structural engineer's spec (typically #2 SPF or better)
Lumber stored off ground on bearers, covered with breathable tarps, stacked by member type for efficient crane or manual set
Engineered lumber (LVL, I-joists, PSL) confirmed on delivery ticket against structural engineer's beam and header schedule; no substitutions without EOR sign-off
Window and exterior door rough opening headers and support hardware on-site and cross-referenced to window/door schedule on approved plans
Structural hardware package (holdowns, straps, joist hangers, post caps, anchor bolt plates) on-site and verified against structural engineer's hardware schedule
Treated sill plate lumber (min. 0.40 CCA or approved equivalent per IRC R317) on-site and confirmed pressure-treated stamp visible
Sill seal gasket material on-site for full perimeter installation under all bottom plates bearing on concrete
Framing crew lead confirmed start date in writing; crew size and composition verified as sufficient for schedule (crew size documented in superintendent's log)
Framing foreman has reviewed approved structural plans, engineer's hardware schedule, and anchor bolt layout before mobilization
Crane or telehandler reserved and delivery/pickup dates confirmed if required by structural plan (two-story, heavy timber, long LVL spans)
Framing sub's certificate of insurance (GL + WC) current and on file; expiration dates checked against project duration
Framing schedule milestone dates communicated to MEP subs (rough-in follows frame) and confirmed they are still holding their reserved slots
Superintendent site walk scheduled with framing foreman the morning of Day 1 to review plan set, critical dims, and quality expectations before work begins

Superintendent has personally walked the slab and verified no condition exists that would prevent compliant framing (cracked concrete, mis-set anchors, out-of-square footprint) — deficiencies resolved or EOR-approved before proceeding
Project manager notified in writing (text or email) that all Pre-framing items are closed and framing is authorized to begin
Baseline framing photos taken and uploaded to project Drive folder (all four elevations of slab, anchor bolt layout, staged materials)
Any open RFIs or plan discrepancies identified during Pre-framing review submitted to architect and resolved before Day 1 of framing
